review_title: By far, the best solution for web accessibility
author: Sebastian C.
pros: It was extremely easy to install and it's a very well-thought-out product as it's very intuitive to use for website visitors. Also, you can also personalize it to the branding of your website so it won't stick out like a sore thumb.
cons: I didn't really face any inconvenience so far.

review_title: Best Solution For Compliance WCAG and ADA both international and domesticly.
description: Overall setup and install was a breeze. Maintenance has been easy and their live support has been very quick and very knowledgeable. I have experience working with many different ADA compliance plugins and services, and Accessibe has been the best service I have worked with.
author: Joe S.
pros: AccessiBe was an extremely easy to install service within my Shopify Plus store. The features and functionality are extensive and completely covers are of our ADA compliance WCAG requirement needs for our niche fashion DTC / B2C e-commerce store. The price point was very reasonable for the services provided.
cons: There are some complexities within the granular details of WCAG and ADA requirements. Like what each image alt tag will be, and whether we are 100% covered from any litigation regarding our ADA Compliance. Covering all bases with no wiggle room for loopholes is an exercise in futility that I am not sure any service can completely do. AccessiBe does well with this, but there is still room to improve.

review_title: BEWARE Terrible customer service
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Intuitive tool for website accessibility, easy to use. My review is mostly highlighting the (most likely) difficulty to cancel a subscription and lack of transparent pricing which I've seen elsewhere that I wish I paid more attention to.
cons: BEWARE Terrible customer service. I don't write bad reviews. In my 16 years in marketing as a Director/C-level Exec, I have never written a bad review. I went out of my way to write a TrustPilot review and create an account because of how appalled I am by my experience. I was charged $389 for a license renewal for a website that doesn't work/doesn't exist. Reached out to customer service within the same day to work through it and was told there was "no way" this could be reversed. For the record, I've been an Accessibe "partner" for over 4 years for other website referrals. As a small business, $400 is a lot of money, and find the lack of empathy or willingness to support this situation enough to warn other vendors and to take my business elsewhere.

review_title: Easy to implement and gets the job done!
author: Mauricio G.
pros: It's a very easy to implement tool on a website and provides the necessary functionality to be compliant with high accessibility standards. It mitigates any legal risks associated with accessibility issues.
cons: Haven't tested all their features, I think they could improve the documentation on features and how to implement.

review_title: campmor review
author: Daniel J.
pros: cost and ease of use and comunication with accessibe
cons: as of now we have no cons we are happy with the product
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Beware of Accessibe’s Auto Renew Policy
description: My overall experience was disappointing. I began with Accessibe hoping to forge a mutually beneficial partnership. It is ending in a potential legal dispute.
author: Julie J.
pros: The widget code is easy to install on a website. The icon on the website’s home page makes all users feel like their experience is important to the owners of the website. I think the company does an excellent job putting up a great front for a good business. They built a solid brand in the accessibility space at a pivotal time in the industry.
cons: Their auto-renew policy. They automatically renewed us for 14 client licenses for a full year. There was no notification that was sent out prior to renewal and those clients were no longer with us. Accessibe refused to even address my support request to at least prorate and credit the unused portion of the year. I am astounded. I want to make sure that everyone knows there is a new law in the state of New York (where the company is domiciled) that declares automatically renewing contracts unenforceable if no notice of the renewal was provided within 15-30 days prior.

review_title: Lack of Transparent Pricing
description: I was not pleased with my experience at all. I felt the pricing was not transparent and it was difficult to cancel the subscription.
author: Eva B.
pros: Accessibe technically made my website ADA compliant.
cons: I was very disappointed increased after I paid up front for a year. I was billed an extra $900.

review_title: Interferes with site functionality
description: At first, I was happy to see that my site was compliant with the WCAG 2.1 standard at the AA level when I installed the widget and ran their auditing tool. However, I tested the site with other auditing tools and it showed up as being non-compliant. I spoke to an actual auditor and he confirmed that Accessibe won't make your site compliant with the standard. This completely contradicts what Accessibe's salesperson had told me.I then notiecd that, because of the widget, filling out forms and selecting a checkbox were now mandatory on my site. All of them shoud have been optional and ticking the checkbox completely changed the results for most searches. This behaviour was noticed on PC only and not mobile. This was an egregious flaw in Accessibe's widget and should have been corrected immediately. However, their technical support told me that I could make changes to my code and that the widget forced this change to make things easier for blind people - I asked them to explain how that is possible and they didn't reply. I had a look at the code and offered them a solution that would have taken seconds to implement on their end, but they took no action.I was contacted by multiple sales and support staff asking me if I was happy with the product and if I was ready to sign up. I had the impression that they lacked structure internally and didn't have a formal process for tracking their relationships with (potential) customers. Maybe the developers never received my feedback?
author: Damian P.
pros: The widget offers some nice features for users to modify the appearance of the site.Very easy to implement and customisation was easy.The widget will make your site partially compliant with WCAG 2.1 at the AA level.They gave an extention on my trial while waiting for their support staff to iron out issues.Works with PC and mobile.
cons: Despite their claims (both on their website and via a video call), this will not guarantee full compliance with WCAG 2.1 to the AA level.The widget interferes with site functionality on PC.Technical support somewhat responsive but very, very poor.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: So Easy To Be Compliant!
description: It was soooo easy to get in touch with someone, set up a demo and get the resources we needed to integrate ASAP. The demo was very helpful, as we were not sure how this compared to the competition. Nir Lavi did an excellent job explaining everything, and made it a no brainer decision to go with AccessiBe.
author: Alicia D.
pros: We love that this is a comprehensive solution for accessibility on our website. It's not lacking any features compared to the competition, and has great support perks in case you run into any legal trouble. The audit they provided made it so easy to see where we can improve, but it's so nice to have the AI fill in the gaps until we can make changes.
cons: Nothing yet, it's only been a couple weeks now. Everything seems to be working perfectly!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Simple and Affordable; Some Hiccups with Support
description: It has been a simple experience and relationship which generally accomplished the goal of achieving a basic level of accessibility compliance.
author: Mark K.
pros: This was very simple to implement via Google Tag Manager and I very quickly accomplished the basic task of adding a compliance tool to my websites.
cons: The audit tool did not work initially and it took months for the right support person to give me corrective suggestions. After that it worked smoothly.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

pros: It's easy to work with right out of the box, and we were able to easily make a couple of small tweaks to make it work for our site.
cons: So far, nothing. We haven't used it for more than a month or so but it seems to have checked all of the boxes right out of the gate.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Was good and satisfies our requirement. But after years of subscription, switched to an alternative due to price hike.
author: Sam N.
pros: The product is easy to install on the website and have proper documentation
cons: The product subscription price was increased.

review_title: accessiBe is the best online accessibility solution for SMBs
description: Providing web accessibility is critical. Potential clients can be lost because of an inaccessible website or app. It's about doing the right thing in the long run, though. The information and material you provide should be accessible to anybody, anywhere, at any time. Assured by AccessiBe, we can rest easy knowing this to be the case.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: You don't have to spend a lot of time, money, or effort getting your business to the greatest possible level of online accessibility, thanks to accessiBe. There were a few other options we considered, but acccessiBe was the only one that fit our budget for this trip. It was simple to set up, and they'll make any necessary adjustments to keep us reachable. The audits are quite helpful, and the user interface is simple to navigate. We use AccessiBe since it ensures that your company users may access your website. It will monitor the site and alert us of any changes to the code that are required for it to pass. We can stay on top of accessibility standards changes if we have a technology that does it automatically.
cons: The domain name should be double-checked during the accessiBe plugin installation process, just to be safe. If the domain name is misspelled, the accessiBe plugin will not work. Using AccessiBe hasn't shown any notable drawbacks so far. We only needed a translation program for the final step. Although AccessiBe could have done more to help with translations, I applaud them for focusing on what they do best.

review_title: Straightforward setup and great information for new clients
description: accessiBe really helped the company to become compliant quickly and gave them confidence as they consulted with their legal team about their ADA/WCAG compliance status
author: Garret M.
pros: The ease of setup and integration with CMS web tools like Wix and WordPress were great. Additionally, the wealth of information provided about ADA/WCAG proved to be helpful in explaining the needs and compliance solutions to the company.
cons: The cost seemed a high to the client; however, in explaining the cost to do a more custom/hands-on solution with custom code and constant modification, it became clear that this was actually a very cost-effective solution.

review_title: Easy, Supportive Accessibility and Compliance Access
description: Everyone who engages with the platform finds it to be a significant upgrade that will allow us to passively but powerfully improve user experience and ensure we are not turning away prospective clients.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: An easy, fast, and reliable way to begin prioritizing website accessibility and site visitor needs and experiences.
cons: The only downside is that there is a financial commitment associated with the product for for-profit businesses, but the product was free for our nonprofit serving the disability community.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Get ADA compliant NOW!
description: Overall my experience with accessiBe has been excellent. The ease of use and setup has been unmatched compared to any other software regarding accessibility.
author: Joy L.
pros: This software makes it SO EASY to set up ADA compliance without an excessive need to go over the front end UX for any website.
cons: The software can be difficult to sell to people who are not as invested in their digital presence. If there was a very cheap option for people who have few page or one page sites I can see a bigger appeal for accessibe

review_title: Easiest and most comprehensive web accessibility solution in the market
description: Really happy with it, easiest way possible to be ADA compliant and accessible.
author: Andre G.
pros: Remarkably easy to install and integrate, even for websites that are above average in terms of design complexity. The UI is intuitive and easy to use, making adjustments a breeze even for those who aren't particularly tech-savvy​.
cons: The white label aspect isn't obvious, even it is a great functionality, so better documentation around would be better.

review_title: Great Tool but their pricing model could be improved
author: Gina C.
pros: I loved everything about this tool. It was easy to install into our app, it offered a lot of functionality and managing our compliance and pulling audits and reports was easy.
cons: We had to cancel our subscription because their pricing model is based off of papers that Google crawls for your domain. We only had Accesibe in our app, not our public website, however since our public website has 10s of 1000s of pages in Google, we were being pushed to their large Enterprise pricing model when our app with the actual widget embedded would have allowed us to remain in their lower tier of pricing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
